title: Take The Plunge 💧| Cinematic FPV Drone Dives Down Waterfall (📹: IG / thankyou.rogers) #drone
creator: Drone Racing League (YouTube Shorts feed; original footage credited to IG creator thankyou.rogers)
notes: CANDIDATE-SOURCED, INDEPENDENTLY RE-VERIFIED THIS SESSION -- one exemplar added to the waterfall/river-landscape subgenre bucket (FPV cinematic style). A prior agent's candidate JSON proposed this URL (https://www.youtube.com/shorts/Dk8QYra2bK0); per this task's step 1 requirement, it was NOT trusted verbatim -- it was re-verified live in this session via YouTube's oEmbed endpoint (curl 'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/shorts/Dk8QYra2bK0&format=json'), which returned title 'Take The Plunge 💧| Cinematic FPV Drone Dives Down Waterfall (📹: IG / thankyou.rogers) #drone' and author_name 'Drone Racing League' (author_url https://www.youtube.com/@Thedroneracingleague) -- an exact field-for-field match to the candidate's title and creator, so this session's own primary-source check corroborates rather than merely repeats the prior agent's claim. A direct curl fetch of the raw watch-page HTML for the Shorts URL (with a desktop User-Agent header) returned ~1.59MB of markup; a case-insensitive grep for 'creative commons', 'licensedForReuse', 'cc-by', and 'Attribution license' found zero matches anywhere in the page, corroborating the prior agent's WebFetch finding of 'no CC marking, only standard Google LLC copyright footer.' Per this project's stated default (assume all-rights-reserved unless the uploader's license setting is directly confirmed otherwise), license_category is set to 'all-rights-reserved' -- determination method: absence of any explicit CC marking in the raw page source, identical to every other record in this pack. Consequently local_media_path stays null and no video file was downloaded or persisted anywhere in this repo. duration is left null: neither the oEmbed response (which does not carry a duration field) nor a grep of the raw page HTML for 'lengthSeconds'/'approxDurationMs' JSON keys (the ytInitialPlayerResponse blob is present in the markup but did not expose those keys in extractable form via this session's fetch) yielded a runtime -- rather than estimate a plausible YouTube Shorts length (well under 60s by format), this field stays unset pending a real YouTube Data API call. A supplementary WebSearch this session ('"Drone Racing League" YouTube subscribers channel') independently corroborates that Drone Racing League is an established, ~1.6-3M-subscriber professional drone-sports media brand (channel ID UCiVmHW7d57ICmEf9WGIp1CA, founded 2015, the world's professional FPV drone-racing league) -- a genuine, disclosed curation/reach signal for the re-posting channel, though it says nothing about the original creator's (thankyou.rogers) own production process, since DRL is amplifying someone else's footage rather than having produced or juried it. A second WebSearch ('"thankyou.rogers" instagram drone FPV') confirmed the credited creator's Instagram account exists (instagram.com/thankyou.rogers, bio 'Ty | Travel | FPV'), consistent with the video's IG-credit caption, but surfaced no further production detail (no drone/camera model, no resolution, no follower count visible in search-snippet form). Only one Drone Racing League exemplar is retained in this pack (per the prior agent's note, two sibling clips from the same channel -- a mountain river dive and a second waterfall dive -- were dropped) to avoid over-representing a single re-posting channel. scores (sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness) are scores_provenance: manually_estimated placeholder judgments, NOT measurements from Capability 1's actual scoring functions (scoring_sharpness.py/scoring_exposure.py/scoring_motion_smoothness.py) -- no source file was ever downloaded to run those functions against, per the spec's no-bulk-download constraint. Calibration reasoning: sharpness=0.76 and exposure=0.74 sit mid-pack (comparable to bbc-earth-aerial.json's 0.78/0.75 and skypixel-landscape.json's 0.78/0.75) rather than at this pack's top tier (dronestagram-feature.json's 0.85/0.8, skypixel11_africa_unseen's 0.8/0.8) -- there is no confirmed drone/camera model, resolution, or juried contest placement for this specific clip (unlike the DJI-model-tagged or SkyPixel-contest-winning exemplars), but re-posting by an established professional media brand (Drone Racing League) is a real, if modest, polish/curation signal above an entirely unvetted personal account (contrast instagram-elisacappelletti-monument-valley.json's 0.74/0.73 and valsoiapg-gran-sasso-alpine.json's 0.75/0.72, both un-curated personal reels). motion_smoothness=0.80 is scored above sharpness/exposure, at the high end of this pack's range: the title's own 'Cinematic' framing plus the well-documented FPV-freestyle convention of applying gyro-based post-stabilization (e.g. ReelSteady/Gyroflow) to raw FPV footage to produce a smooth, controlled dive shot despite the drone's aggressive real flight path are treated as a genuine (if qualitative) motion-smoothness signal -- reasoned from a documented genre convention, not an arbitrary guess; no frame-level motion measurement was performed. composite_score IS a real computation: it was produced this session by calling this repo's own drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.composite.compute_composite_score with these three manually-estimated inputs (sharpness=0.76, exposure=0.74, motion_smoothness=0.80, composition=None) and drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.weights.default_weights() via '.venv/bin/python3' in this session. default_weights() with no explicit version argument returned weights_version 'default-v2' (sharpness=exposure=motion_smoothness=composition=0.25); because composition's score is None (this pack's Milestone-1 Capability-1 convention), compute_composite_score's defensive None-skipping behavior excludes composition from both the weighted sum and the renormalization denominator, so the result reduces to the equal-weighted average of the three present signals: (0.76+0.74+0.80)/3 = 0.7666666666666666 (rounded to 0.7667 here), confirmed by actually running the function this session rather than hand-computed.

editorial_style.pacing_notes: format='short-form-reel' is grounded in two structural facts obtained via this session's own YouTube oEmbed fetch, not a guess from the title alone: (1) the source URL itself is a /shorts/ path -- YouTube's own platform-level designation for its short-form vertical product; (2) the oEmbed JSON response's embed dimensions (height=200, width=113) describe a portrait/vertical aspect ratio, consistent with vertical short-form content and inconsistent with the 16:9 landscape aspect ratio of this pack's long-form cinematic exemplars. No live playback or scrubbing of the video was attempted in this session: claude-in-chrome browser-automation tools are available in this environment but were not invoked, consistent with this pack's existing precedent for reel-style exemplars (see instagram-elisacappelletti-monument-valley.json's pacing_notes) of not exercising an interactive browser session for a single-record bounded verification task. A WebSearch this session ('"thankyou.rogers" instagram drone FPV') surfaced only the credited creator's Instagram profile/bio ('Ty | Travel | FPV') -- confirming the account exists and matches the video's IG-credit caption -- with no commentary describing this specific clip's cut count, shot length, or transition style; a prior agent's broader text-provenance search pass (YouTube Shorts page, YouTube watch page, the creator's Instagram profile, and targeted searches for a waterfall-dive post from this creator) likewise found no genuine creator description, interview, or press coverage of this video's editing style, only generic genre commentary about FPV waterfall-dive videos in general, plus one unrelated Instagram post credited to a different creator that does not match thankyou.rogers and was correctly rejected as inadmissible. estimated_cut_count, avg_shot_length_seconds, and transition_styles_observed therefore stay null/empty rather than guessed -- no genuine playback or text-sourced basis for those three fields exists in or before this session.
